spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Mobile-first tactical HUD. Maximize camera viewport by pushing controls to the edges and bottom. Use floating glassmorphic panels for non-obstructive data display.
spacing_and_layout.grid_system: The Control Room Grid: High density, gap-4, full-width bottom drawers for actions.

surface_strategies.hud_panels: Crystal Glassmorphism: bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-xl border border-white/10 shadow-[0_8px_32px_rgba(0,0,0,0.5)] rounded-xl p-4
surface_strategies.action_bars: Solid flat panels at bottom: bg-[#0A0A0A] border-t border-white/10 p-6 rounded-t-3xl
surface_strategies.data_pills: Tactical outline: bg-transparent border border-white/20 rounded-full px-3 py-1 font-mono text-xs text-white

motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Tactical crisp transitions. Hover scales slightly (+1.02) and boosts border brightness to white/40.
motion_and_interactions.active_states: High contrast feedback. Active pins glow with shadow-[0_0_12px_rgba(255,69,0,0.8)].
motion_and_interactions.ar_interactions: Reticle shrinks on valid plane detection. Line drawing uses a neon trailing effect.

components_strategy.buttons: Large touch targets (min h-14) for outdoor/gloved use. Primary button uses solid #FF4500, no shadow. Secondary buttons are outline (border-white/20).
components_strategy.inputs: Shadcn based, but heavily customized to look tactical. bg-black border-white/20 text-white focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#FF4500].
components_strategy.navigation: Floating bottom tab bar or side rail (landscape). Icon-heavy, text-light.
components_strategy.map_view: Dark mode ESRI satellite tiles. Overlays use bright neon colors (Volt Blue, Orange) for extreme contrast against natural terrain.

login_background.description: Abstract satellite topography view. Use as a darkened background for the Google OAuth login screen.

empty_state_map.description: Abstract dark terrain to use behind map placeholders or empty states.

accessibility.contrast: Crucial for outdoor visibility. All text on HUDs must have text-shadow to ensure readability against bright sky or dark ground camera feeds.
accessibility.touch_targets: Minimum 48x48px for all interactive HUD elements.
accessibility.testing_attributes: All buttons, tabs, and map markers MUST use data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='ar-drop-pin-btn').

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Do not use default white/light UI. The app must be dark mode by default to save battery and reduce glare for outdoor surveying.
2. For the AR Camera view, the background MUST be transparent to allow the video feed to show through. Wrap UI elements in absolute positioned glassmorphic containers.
3. Use `lucide-react` for iconography (Crosshair, Map, Compass, MapPin).
4. For data readouts (coordinates, distance, bearing), ALWAYS use the IBM Plex Mono font class to ensure numerical alignment.
5. Implement a bottom sheet or floating action button layout for mobile ergonomics. Do not place critical actions at the top of the screen.
6. Use Tailwind text-shadow utilities (or custom classes) on HUD text so it doesn't get lost when the camera points at white clouds or bright objects.
7. For mapping, use React-Leaflet or similar with a dark satellite tile layer. Ensure the map UI matches the tactical aesthetic (no standard Google Maps white borders).
8. Expose data-testid on every interactive element to facilitate automated testing.
